title: Graph ADT Components and Their Relationships
description: A visual breakdown of the core elements of a Graph Abstract Data Type, showing how vertices (nodes) and edges (connections) form the fundamental structure and relate to each other.
relationships: Directed Edges have a specific direction (from source to destination)
Undirected Edges have no direction and represent a two-way connection
Weighted Edges have a value (weight/cost) associated with them
Two vertices are Adjacent if they are connected by an edge
A Path is a sequence of edges connecting two vertices
A Cycle is a path that starts and ends at the same vertex
